marketState: neutral, leaning bullish on weather/COT extremes offset by bearish storage and daily-weekly technical trend
summary: Front-month gas is trading almost exactly at fair value across all horizons ($2.923-$3.017 fair vs $2.934 front, all status FAIR), so there is no valuation-band edge right now. The 8-16 day weather window is the standout signal: demand is +38% vs 5-year norms and rangePos is above the historical max, a strong bullish heat driver into August. Managed money is net short at only the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, a stretched positioning extreme that raises squeeze risk if heat/demand keeps building. Working against this, storage sits +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, and technicals are bearish on the daily and weekly timeframes (EMA50<200, negative MACD). Seasonality is mildly supportive (price running -4.3pp below its typical-year path, 69% historical odds of being higher in 1 and 4 weeks) but flagged Low reliability, so the overall read is a cautiously bullish medium-term tilt against a fairly priced, technically weak near-term tape.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; scale out 50% at TP1, trail remainder toward TP2
longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is +38% vs 5y and above the historical max range going into peak cooling season, managed money is near a 5y-low percentile net short (8th pct) leaving room for short covering, and price is running below its seasonal median (-13.9% YTD vs -9.6% median) with a 69% historical hit-rate for higher prices 4 weeks out. Storage is a genuine headwind (+180 Bcf vs 5y, bearish build surprise), so this is sized as a medium-conviction, not high-conviction, long.
longTerm.invalidation: Daily/weekly close below $2.60, the 8-16D heat anomaly fading back toward normal, and/or storage surplus vs 5y continuing to widen would invalidate the bullish case

shortTerm.rationale: Front price sits dead-center FAIR across now/+7D/+14D fair-value windows (spreads of just 1-2 cents), near-term (1-7D) weather demand is only +1.4% vs 5y and mid-range, and technicals are conflicting - 5m/15m RSI near oversold (33-38) argues against chasing a short while the daily/weekly trend is clearly bearish (EMA50<200, negative MACD) arguing against a long. There is no near-term valuation or trend edge to trade.
shortTerm.trigger: Daily close below $2.75 (below fair-value lower-mid, confirming the bearish daily/weekly trend) would open a short-term SHORT; a daily close above $3.05 with 1h/4h RSI reclaiming 50 and EMA structure turning up would support a short-term LONG aligned with the weather thesis

keyDrivers: 8-16D weather demand +38% vs 5y, above the historical max range - strong bullish heat catalyst into August
Managed money net short at the 8th percentile of its 5y range - stretched positioning, squeeze risk
Price running below seasonal median (-13.9% YTD vs -9.6%) with 69%/69% odds of being higher in 1/4 weeks
Storage +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise - caps upside
Daily and weekly technical trend bearish (EMA50<200, negative MACD) across 1h-1w timeframes
risks: Seasonality reliability is flagged Low (63pp 10-90 spread) - the probability tilts are noisy and can flip quickly
Storage surplus and bearish build could keep pressuring price even if weather stays hot, blunting the long thesis
Price is exactly FAIR across every fair-value horizon - no valuation cushion, so moves can whipsaw against either side
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
